data.title: Binance partners with BBVA in Spain for secure crypto custody following FTX incident, enhancing trust in exchanges.
data.description: With trust in cryptocurrency exchanges still lacking, Binance's recent partnership with BBVA for custody services signifies a move towards more conventional financial security measures. This deal is a significant step in bolstering confidence in the industry, where approximately 58% of consumers remain wary of utilizing exchanges due to concerns related to security and transparency. By teaming up with BBVA, a major player in the traditional banking sector, Binance aims to enhance the safety of its users' assets and promote a more secure environment for trading digital assets. This collaboration underlines a growing trend in the crypto space as exchanges seek to integrate established financial institutions to improve credibility and provide clients with added peace of mind.

data.title: July in Review: 15 Top Finance Press Releases highlight latest financial news. AI adoption impacted by earnings.
data.description: The latest financial updates from PR Newswire highlight the profound impact of quarterly earnings on the adoption of corporate AI. This report was released on August 8, 2025, in New York. The sheer volume of press releases each month poses a challenge in staying abreast of key developments in the financial landscape.

data.title: Gray Media to pay $0.08/share in quarterly cash dividend to shareholders on Sep 30, 2025; record date: Sep 15, 2025.
data.description: Today, Gray Media, Inc. (NYSE: GTN) based in Atlanta, declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per share for its common stock and Class A common stock. This dividend will be distributed on September 30, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of September 15, 2025.

data.title: Tempus AI, Inc. (NASDAQ: TEM) reported 2nd quarter 2025 revenue of $314.6 million, an 89.6% increase YoY. Genomics revenue rose 115.3% YoY to $241.8 million.
data.description: In Chicago, Tempus AI, Inc. (NASDAQ: TEM), a tech firm driving AI's use in improving precision medicine and patient care, shared its financials for the quarter finishing on June 30, 2025. They saw a notable 89.6% year-over-year revenue jump, reaching $314.6 million for the second quarter. Genomics revenue specifically surged by 115.3% year-over-year to $241.8 million, propelled by robust year-over-year volume growth in Oncology (26%) and Hereditary (32%) testing. Additionally, Data and services revenue experienced a 35% increase.

data.title: Docebo Inc. reports Q2 2025 results, exceeding revenue and profitability targets, in still-uncertain market conditions.
data.description: In Toronto, Docebo Inc. (NASDAQ: DCBO; TSX:DCBO) disclosed its financial figures for the three and six months concluded on June 30, 2025. The leading learning platform provider, grounded in artificial intelligence (AI) and innovation, surpassed expectations with strong results in revenue and profitability for the quarter. The company maintained disciplined execution in the face of ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties. All figures are reported in US dollars unless specified otherwise.

data.title: Earn crypto without trading: Diversify with crypto index funds & ETFs for passive income. Minimize trading, maximize gains.
data.description: Investing in crypto index funds and exchange-traded funds (ETFs) can be a way to generate passive income while diversifying your portfolio and reducing the need for frequent trading. These funds typically track the performance of a specific cryptocurrency index or a selection of cryptocurrencies. By investing in a diversified basket of digital assets, investors can reduce risk and exposure to high volatility. This passive investment approach can be particularly appealing to those looking to earn returns without actively managing their holdings. It offers a way to participate in the crypto market's potential growth while minimizing the time and effort needed for day-to-day trading.

data.title: Lamar Advertising's Q2 2025 results show net revenues of $579.3 million, net income of $155.0 million, adjusted EBITDA of $278.4 million. Full-year guidance revised.
data.description: Lamar Advertising Company reported its financial results for the second quarter and first half of 2025. In the second quarter, net revenues were $579.3 million, net income was $155.0 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $278.4 million. For the first six months, net revenues were $1.08 billion, net income was $294.2 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $488.6 million. Lamar's CEO noted revenue growth and completion of a milestone acquisition. The company slightly revised its full-year diluted AFFO per share guidance. Second-quarter highlights included a 2.5% increase in net revenues, a 12.7% increase in net income, and a 2.5% increase in adjusted EBITDA. The company had $363.0 million in total liquidity as of June 30, 2025. Lamar also issued Common Units as part of an acquisition, and revised its 2025 guidance. Investors were cautioned about risks and uncertainties, and Lamar discussed the use of non-GAAP financial measures to evaluate performance. The company's stock is listed on NASDAQ under the symbol LAMR.

data.title: loanDepot promotes Tom Fiddler to President of Retail Lending & Dan Peña to President of Partnership Lending, to drive growth.Char: 100
data.description: In Irvine, California, loanDepot, Inc. (NYSE: LDI) disclosed the promotion of two executives to strategic roles aiming to foster growth and profitability. Tom Fiddler has been appointed as President of Retail Lending, tasked with overseeing all Retail loan production. This move is set to bolster the company's efforts in boosting top-line growth, achieving profitability, and reclaiming market share. Fiddler, previously the Executive Vice President of Retail, will now take on expanded responsibilities in this newly created position.

data.title: American Strategic Investment Co. (NYSE: NYC) reported Q2 2025 revenue of $12.2M vs $15.8M in 2024, with a net loss.
data.description: In a recent press release, American Strategic Investment Co. (NYSE: NYC), known as ASIC, shared its financial performance for the second quarter of 2025. The company, which holds a collection of commercial real estate properties in New York City, reported revenue of $12.2 million for this quarter. This marks a decrease from the $15.8 million recorded in the same period in 2024, mainly due to the sale of 9 Times Square in the previous year. The net loss attributable to shareholders for the second quarter of 2025 was not disclosed in the text.

data.title: Sanuwave's Q2 FY2025 revenues hit $10.2 million, up 42% from $7.2 million in Q2 FY2024. Company aims for $12-$12.7 million in Q3 FY2025.
data.description: Sanuwave Health, Inc. reported Q2 2025 revenues of $10.2 million, a 42% increase from $7.2 million in Q2 2024, marking their highest Q2 quarterly revenues. The Q2 2025 gross margin was 78.3% (up from 73.2% in Q2 2024) with an operating income of $1.9 million (versus $2.0 million in Q2 2024). The company sold 116 UltraMist systems in Q2 2025, up from 72 in Q2 2024. Q2 2025 included equity compensation costs of $1.1 million. The company forecasts revenues of $12-$12.7 million for Q3 2025 and projects a full year 2025 revenue of $48-$50 million (a 47-53% increase from 2024). Adjusted EBITDA for Q2 2025 was $3.4 million, up from $1.5 million in Q2 2024. CEO Morgan Frank anticipates breakout performance in Q4 2025 and 2026 after building up sales and commercial operations teams. The Company's total assets amounted to $33,046 at June 30, 2025.

data.title: Strawberry Fields REIT reports strong Q2 results: $20.0M FFO, $18.9M AFFO, $8.7M net income. Acquisitions boost rental income to $37.9M. CEO optimistic about company's growth.
data.description: Strawberry Fields REIT, Inc. reported its Q2 results for 2025 with financial highlights including 100% of contractual rents collected. They acquired a skilled nursing facility in Texas for $11.5 million and nine more facilities in Missouri for $59 million. The company also issued $90 million in Series B Bonds and used $30 million to pay down debt. For the quarter, FFO was $20 million, AFFO $18.9 million, net income $8.7 million, and rental income $37.9 million. In the first six months of 2025, FFO was $38.2 million, AFFO $35.2 million, net income $15.7 million, and rental income $75.2 million. The CEO noted strong earnings and growth opportunities. Net income increased to $8.7 million in Q2 from $7.2 million in 2024, primarily from higher rental income. For the six months, net income was $15.7 million in 2025 versus $13 million in 2024. The article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: 13 top press releases in July, incl. Princess Cruises packages, BuzzBallz pool, and inaugural TIME100 Creators List. NEW YORK, Aug. 8, 2025.
data.description: New offerings have been launched by Princess Cruises, BuzzBallz has released a new pool product, and TIME100 Creators List has made its debut. The news broke in New York on August 8, 2025. Amid the influx of press releases flooding PR Newswire monthly, it's a challenge for journalists to stay updated. The latest updates include thousands of new packages from Princess Cruises, a new pool product from BuzzBallz, and the introduction of the inaugural TIME100 Creators List.

data.title: ChipMOS reported its July 2025 revenue. Stock Exchange: 8150,Taiwan Stock Exchange, Nasdaq: IMOS.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, in Hsinchu, ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES INC. (Taiwan Stock Exchange: 8150 and Nasdaq: IMOS), a top provider of outsourced semiconductor assembly and test services ("OSAT"), released its unaudited consolidated financial results.
